104	
105	/**
106	 * Prepare audios for further tasks.
107	 * @param {AudioPipelineInputs} audios audios to prepare.
108	 * @param {number} sampling_rate sampling rate of the audios.
109	 * @returns {Promise<Float32Array[]>} The preprocessed audio data.
110	 * @private
111	 */
112	async function prepareAudios(audios, sampling_rate) {
113	    if (!Array.isArray(audios)) {
114	        audios = [audios];
115	    }
116	
117	    return await Promise.all(audios.map(x => {
118	        if (typeof x === 'string' || x instanceof URL) {
119	            return read_audio(x, sampling_rate);
120	        } else if (x instanceof Float64Array) {
121	            return new Float32Array(x);
122	        }
123	        return x;
124	    }));
125	}

135	/**
136	 * Helper function to convert list [xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax] into object { "xmin": xmin, ... }
137	 * @param {number[]} box The bounding box as a list.
138	 * @param {boolean} asInteger Whether to cast to integers.
139	 * @returns {BoundingBox} The bounding box as an object.
140	 * @private
141	 */
142	function get_bounding_box(box, asInteger) {
143	    if (asInteger) {
144	        box = box.map(x => x | 0);
145	    }
146	    const [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax] = box;
147

390	        super(options);
391	    }

393	    /** @type {TokenClassificationPipelineCallback} */
394	    async _call(texts, {
395	        ignore_labels = ['O'],
396	    } = {}) {
397	
398	        const isBatched = Array.isArray(texts);
399	
400	        // Run tokenization
401	        const model_inputs = this.tokenizer(isBatched ? texts : [texts], {
402	            padding: true,
403	            truncation: true,
404	        });
405	
406	        // Run model
407	        const outputs = await this.model(model_inputs)
408	
409	        const logits = outputs.logits;
410	        // @ts-expect-error TS2339
411	        const id2label = this.model.config.id2label;
412	
413	        const toReturn = [];
414	        for (let i = 0; i < logits.dims[0]; ++i) {
415	            const ids = model_inputs.input_ids[i];
416	            const batch = logits[i];
417	
418	            // List of tokens that aren't ignored
419	            const tokens = [];
420	            for (let j = 0; j < batch.dims[0]; ++j) {
421	                const tokenData = batch[j];
422	                const topScoreIndex = max(tokenData.data)[1];
423	
424	                const entity = id2label ? id2label[topScoreIndex] : `LABEL_${topScoreIndex}`;
425	                if (ignore_labels.includes(entity)) {
426	                    // We predicted a token that should be ignored. So, we skip it.
427	                    continue;
428	                }
429	
430	                // TODO add option to keep special tokens?
431	                const word = this.tokenizer.decode([ids[j].item()], { skip_special_tokens: true });
432	                if (word === '') {
433	                    // Was a special token. So, we skip it.
434	                    continue;
435	                }
436	
437	                const scores = softmax(tokenData.data);
438	
439	                tokens.push({
440	                    entity: entity,
441	                    score: scores[topScoreIndex],
442	                    index: j,
443	                    word: word,
444	
445	                    // TODO: Add support for start and end
446	                    // start: null,
447	                    // end: null,
448	                });
449	            }

451	        }
452	        return isBatched ? toReturn : toReturn[0];

7843	export class PretrainedMixin {
7844	    /**
7845	     * Mapping from model type to model class.
7846	     * @type {Map<string, Object>[]}
7847	     */
7848	    static MODEL_CLASS_MAPPINGS = null;
7849	
7850	    /**
7851	     * Whether to attempt to instantiate the base class (`PretrainedModel`) if 
7852	     * the model type is not found in the mapping.
7853	     */
7854	    static BASE_IF_FAIL = false;
7855	
7856	
7857	    /** @type {typeof PreTrainedModel.from_pretrained} */
7858	    static async from_pretrained(pretrained_model_name_or_path, {
7859	        progress_callback = null,
7860	        config = null,
7861	        cache_dir = null,
7862	        local_files_only = false,
7863	        revision = 'main',
7864	        model_file_name = null,
7865	        subfolder = 'onnx',
7866	        device = null,
7867	        dtype = null,
7868	        use_external_data_format = null,
7869	        session_options = {},
7870	    } = {}) {
7871	
7872	        const options = {
7873	            progress_callback,
7874	            config,
7875	            cache_dir,
7876	            local_files_only,
7877	            revision,
7878	            model_file_name,
7879	            subfolder,
7880	            device,
7881	            dtype,
7882	            use_external_data_format,
7883	            session_options,
7884	        }
7885	        options.config = await AutoConfig.from_pretrained(pretrained_model_name_or_path, options);
7886	
7887	        if (!this.MODEL_CLASS_MAPPINGS) {
7888	            throw new Error("`MODEL_CLASS_MAPPINGS` not implemented for this type of `AutoClass`: " + this.name);
7889	        }
7890	        const model_type = options.config.model_type;
7891	        for (const MODEL_CLASS_MAPPING of this.MODEL_CLASS_MAPPINGS) {
7892	            let modelInfo = MODEL_CLASS_MAPPING.get(model_type);
7893	            if (!modelInfo) {
7894	                // As a fallback, we check if model_type is specified as the exact class
7895	                for (const cls of MODEL_CLASS_MAPPING.values()) {
7896	                    if (cls[0] === model_type) {
7897	                        modelInfo = cls;
7898	                        break;
7899	                    }
7900	                }
7901	                if (!modelInfo) continue; // Item not found in this mapping
7902	            }
7903	            return await modelInfo[1].from_pretrained(pretrained_model_name_or_path, options);
7904	        }
7905	
7906	        if (this.BASE_IF_FAIL) {
7907	            if (!(CUSTOM_ARCHITECTURES.has(model_type))) {
7908	                console.warn(`Unknown model class "${model_type}", attempting to construct from base class.`);
7909	            }
7910	            return await PreTrainedModel.from_pretrained(pretrained_model_name_or_path, options);
7911	        } else {
7912	            throw Error(`Unsupported model type: ${model_type}`)
7913	        }
7914	    }
7915	}

4422	     * - A string, the *model id* of a pretrained tokenizer hosted inside a model repo on huggingface.co.
4423	     *   Valid model ids can be located at the root-level, like `bert-base-uncased`, or namespaced under a
4424	     *   user or organization name, like `dbmdz/bert-base-german-cased`.
4425	     * - A path to a *directory* containing tokenizer files, e.g., `./my_model_directory/`.
4426	     * @param {PretrainedTokenizerOptions} options Additional options for loading the tokenizer.
4427	     * 
4428	     * @returns {Promise<PreTrainedTokenizer>} A new instance of the PreTrainedTokenizer class.
4429	     */
4430	    static async from_pretrained(pretrained_model_name_or_path, {
4431	        progress_callback = null,
4432	        config = null,
4433	        cache_dir = null,
4434	        local_files_only = false,
4435	        revision = 'main',
4436	        legacy = null,
4437	    } = {}) {
4438	
4439	        const [tokenizerJSON, tokenizerConfig] = await loadTokenizer(pretrained_model_name_or_path, {
4440	            progress_callback,
4441	            config,
4442	            cache_dir,
4443	            local_files_only,
4444	            revision,
4445	            legacy,
4446	        })
4447	
4448	        // Some tokenizers are saved with the "Fast" suffix, so we remove that if present.
4449	        const tokenizerName = tokenizerConfig.tokenizer_class?.replace(/Fast$/, '') ?? 'PreTrainedTokenizer';

4451	        let cls = this.TOKENIZER_CLASS_MAPPING[tokenizerName];

## Thorough Exploration of `src/pipelines.js` - Transformers.js Library

Based on my complete analysis of the file, here's a comprehensive breakdown of the pipeline architecture:

---

### 1. OVERALL STRUCTURE - File Layout & Imports

**File Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775400268200-c01bcp/src/pipelines.js`

**Key Imports (lines 16-77):**
- **Tokenizers:** `AutoTokenizer`, `PreTrainedTokenizer`
- **Models:** Multiple auto model classes including `AutoModelForDocumentQuestionAnswering`, `AutoModelForVision2Seq`, etc.
- **Processors:** `AutoProcessor`, `Processor`
- **Utilities:** `Callable`, `Tensor`, `RawImage`, `RawAudio`, `softmax`, `topk`, etc.

**Class Hierarchy:**
- Base class: `Pipeline extends Callable` (line 164)
- All specific pipelines extend `Pipeline` with type assertions for their constructor args and callback types

---

### 2. DOCUMENTQUESTIONANSWERINGPIPELINE - Complete Implementation

**Location:** Lines 2704-2779

**Typedefs:**
```javascript
/**
 * @typedef {Object} DocumentQuestionAnsweringSingle
 * @property {string} answer The generated text.
 * @typedef {DocumentQuestionAnsweringSingle[]} DocumentQuestionAnsweringOutput
 *
 * @callback DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineCallback Answer the question given as input by using the document.
 * @param {ImageInput} image The image of the document to use.
 * @param {string} question A question to ask of the document.
 * @param {Partial<import('./generation/configuration_utils.js').GenerationConfig>} [options] Additional keyword arguments to pass along to the generate method of the model.
 * @returns {Promise<DocumentQuestionAnsweringOutput|DocumentQuestionAnsweringOutput[]>} An object (or array of objects) containing the answer(s).
 *
 * @typedef {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs & DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineCallback & Disposable} DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineType
 */
```

**Class Declaration:**
```javascript
export class DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {

    /**
     * Create a new DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline.
     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
     */
    constructor(options) {
        super(options);
    }
```

**_call Method (lines 2742-2778):**
```javascript
    /** @type {DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineCallback} */
    async _call(image, question, generate_kwargs = {}) {

        // NOTE: For now, we only support a batch size of 1

        // Preprocess image
        const preparedImage = (await prepareImages(image))[0];
        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImage);

        // Run tokenization
        const task_prompt = `<s_docvqa><s_question>${question}</s_question><s_answer>`;
        const decoder_input_ids = this.tokenizer(task_prompt, {
            add_special_tokens: false,
            padding: true,
            truncation: true,
        }).input_ids;

        // Run model
        const output = await this.model.generate({
            inputs: pixel_values,
            // @ts-expect-error TS2339
            max_length: this.model.config.decoder.max_position_embeddings,
            decoder_input_ids,
            ...generate_kwargs,
        });

        // Decode output
        const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output))[0];

        // Parse answer
        const match = decoded.match(/<s_answer>(.*?)<\/s_answer>/);
        let answer = null;
        if (match && match.length >= 2) {
            answer = match[1].trim();
        }
        return [{ answer }];
    }
```

**Key Characteristics:**
- **Multimodal:** Takes both image and text (question) inputs
- **Constructor args type:** `TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs` (requires tokenizer AND processor)
- **Processing flow:** Image preprocessing → Processor for pixel values → Tokenizer for question prompt → Model generation → Regex parsing of answer
- **Single batch:** Only supports batch size of 1 (per comment)
- **Output:** Always returns array format `[{ answer: string }]`

---

### 3. IMAGETOTEXTPIPELINE - Complete Implementation

**Location:** Lines 1943-2005

**Typedefs:**
```javascript
/**
 * @typedef {Object} ImageToTextSingle
 * @property {string} generated_text The generated text.
 * @typedef {ImageToTextSingle[]} ImageToTextOutput
 *
 * @callback ImageToTextPipelineCallback Assign labels to the image(s) passed as inputs.
 * @param {ImagePipelineInputs} texts The images to be captioned.
 * @param {Partial<import('./generation/configuration_utils.js').GenerationConfig>} [options] Additional keyword arguments to pass along to the generate method of the model.
 * @returns {Promise<ImageToTextOutput|ImageToTextOutput[]>} An object (or array of objects) containing the generated text(s).
 *
 * @typedef {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs & ImageToTextPipelineCallback & Disposable} ImageToTextPipelineType
 */
```

**Class Declaration:**
```javascript
export class ImageToTextPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => ImageToTextPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {

    /**
     * Create a new ImageToTextPipeline.
     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
     */
    constructor(options) {
        super(options);
    }
```

**_call Method (lines 1985-2004):**
```javascript
    /** @type {ImageToTextPipelineCallback} */
    async _call(images, generate_kwargs = {}) {

        const isBatched = Array.isArray(images);
        const preparedImages = await prepareImages(images);

        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImages);

        const toReturn = [];
        for (const batch of pixel_values) {
            batch.dims = [1, ...batch.dims]
            const output = await this.model.generate({ inputs: batch, ...generate_kwargs });
            const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output), {
                skip_special_tokens: true,
            }).map(x => ({ generated_text: x.trim() }))
            toReturn.push(decoded);
        }

        return isBatched ? toReturn : toReturn[0];
    }
```

**Key Characteristics:**
- **Multimodal:** Takes images as input, generates text (captions/OCR)
- **Constructor args type:** `TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs` (requires tokenizer AND processor)
- **Batching:** Supports both single and batch inputs with `isBatched` flag
- **Processing flow:** Image preparation → Processor for pixel values → Model generation → Tokenizer batch decode
- **Output format:** Preserves batching (returns array for batched input, single object for single input)
- **Batch dimension:** Modifies `batch.dims` to add leading 1: `[1, ...batch.dims]`

---

### 4. SUPPORTED_TASKS Mapping Examples

**Location:** Lines 3096-3390

**Key Examples Showing Registration Pattern:**

**Text-Only Pipeline (text-classification):**
```javascript
"text-classification": {
    "tokenizer": AutoTokenizer,
    "pipeline": TextClassificationPipeline,
    "model": AutoModelForSequenceClassification,
    "default": {
        "model": "Xenova/distilbert-base-uncased-finetuned-sst-2-english",
    },
    "type": "text",
},
```

**Audio-Only Pipeline (audio-classification):**
```javascript
"audio-classification": {
    "pipeline": AudioClassificationPipeline,
    "model": AutoModelForAudioClassification,
    "processor": AutoProcessor,
    "default": {
        "model": "Xenova/wav2vec2-base-superb-ks",
    },
    "type": "audio",
},
```

**Multimodal with Optional Components (text-to-audio):**
```javascript
"text-to-audio": {
    "tokenizer": AutoTokenizer,
    "pipeline": TextToAudioPipeline,
    "model": [AutoModelForTextToWaveform, AutoModelForTextToSpectrogram],
    "processor": [AutoProcessor, /* Some don't use a processor */ null],
    "default": {
        "model": "Xenova/speecht5_tts",
    },
    "type": "text",
},
```

**Reference: ImageToTextPipeline Registration:**
```javascript
"image-to-text": {
    "tokenizer": AutoTokenizer,
    "pipeline": ImageToTextPipeline,
    "model": AutoModelForVision2Seq,
    "processor": AutoProcessor,
    "default": {
        "model": "Xenova/vit-gpt2-image-captioning",
    },
    "type": "multimodal",
},
```

**Reference: DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline Registration:**
```javascript
"document-question-answering": {
    "tokenizer": AutoTokenizer,
    "pipeline": DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline,
    "model": AutoModelForDocumentQuestionAnswering,
    "processor": AutoProcessor,
    "default": {
        "model": "Xenova/donut-base-finetuned-docvqa",
    },
    "type": "multimodal",
},
```

**Key Registration Patterns:**
- Entries are frozen with `Object.freeze()`
- Required properties: `pipeline` (class), `model` (class or array), `default` (object with model name), `type`
- Optional properties: `tokenizer`, `processor` (can be omitted, be an array, or include `null` for optionality)
- Type field: can be `"text"`, `"audio"`, `"image"`, or `"multimodal"`

---

### 5. Pipeline Factory Function

**Location:** Lines 3414-3513

**Complete JSDoc (from lines 3414-3444):**
```javascript
/**
 * Utility factory method to build a `Pipeline` object.
 *
 * @template {PipelineType} T The type of pipeline to return.
 * @param {T} task The task defining which pipeline will be returned. Currently accepted tasks are:
 *  - `"audio-classification"`: will return a `AudioClassificationPipeline`.
 *  - `"automatic-speech-recognition"`: will return a `AutomaticSpeechRecognitionPipeline`.
 *  - `"depth-estimation"`: will return a `DepthEstimationPipeline`.
 *  - `"document-question-answering"`: will return a `DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline`.
 *  - `"feature-extraction"`: will return a `FeatureExtractionPipeline`.
 *  - `"fill-mask"`: will return a `FillMaskPipeline`.
 *  - `"image-classification"`: will return a `ImageClassificationPipeline`.
 *  - `"image-segmentation"`: will return a `ImageSegmentationPipeline`.
 *  - `"image-to-text"`: will return a `ImageToTextPipeline`.
 *  - `"object-detection"`: will return a `ObjectDetectionPipeline`.
 *  - `"question-answering"`: will return a `QuestionAnsweringPipeline`.
 *  - `"summarization"`: will return a `SummarizationPipeline`.
 *  - `"text2text-generation"`: will return a `Text2TextGenerationPipeline`.
 *  - `"text-classification"` (alias "sentiment-analysis" available): will return a `TextClassificationPipeline`.
 *  - `"text-generation"`: will return a `TextGenerationPipeline`.
 *  - `"token-classification"` (alias "ner" available): will return a `TokenClassificationPipeline`.
 *  - `"translation"`: will return a `TranslationPipeline`.
 *  - `"translation_xx_to_yy"`: will return a `TranslationPipeline`.
 *  - `"zero-shot-classification"`: will return a `ZeroShotClassificationPipeline`.
 *  - `"zero-shot-audio-classification"`: will return a `ZeroShotAudioClassificationPipeline`.
 *  - `"zero-shot-image-classification"`: will return a `ZeroShotImageClassificationPipeline`.
 *  - `"zero-shot-object-detection"`: will return a `ZeroShotObjectDetectionPipeline`.
 * @param {string} [model=null] The name of the pre-trained model to use. If not specified, the default model for the task will be used.
 * @param {import('./utils/hub.js').PretrainedModelOptions} [options] Optional parameters for the pipeline.
 * @returns {Promise<AllTasks[T]>} A Pipeline object for the specified task.
 * @throws {Error} If an unsupported pipeline is requested.
 */
export async function pipeline(
    task,
    model = null,
    {
        progress_callback = null,
        config = null,
        cache_dir = null,
        local_files_only = false,
        revision = 'main',
        device = null,
        dtype = null,
        subfolder = 'onnx',
        use_external_data_format = null,
        model_file_name = null,
        session_options = {},
    } = {}
)
```

**Function Implementation (lines 3463-3513):**
```javascript
{
    // Helper method to construct pipeline

    // Apply aliases
    // @ts-ignore
    task = TASK_ALIASES[task] ?? task;

    // Get pipeline info
    const pipelineInfo = SUPPORTED_TASKS[task.split('_', 1)[0]];
    if (!pipelineInfo) {
        throw Error(`Unsupported pipeline: ${task}. Must be one of [${Object.keys(SUPPORTED_TASKS)}]`)
    }

    // Use model if specified, otherwise, use default
    if (!model) {
        model = pipelineInfo.default.model
        console.log(`No model specified. Using default model: "${model}".`);
    }

    const pretrainedOptions = {
        progress_callback,
        config,
        cache_dir,
        local_files_only,
        revision,
        device,
        dtype,
        subfolder,
        use_external_data_format,
        model_file_name,
        session_options,
    }

    const classes = new Map([
        ['tokenizer', pipelineInfo.tokenizer],
        ['model', pipelineInfo.model],
        ['processor', pipelineInfo.processor],
    ]);

    // Load model, tokenizer, and processor (if they exist)
    const results = await loadItems(classes, model, pretrainedOptions);
    results.task = task;

    dispatchCallback(progress_callback, {
        'status': 'ready',
        'task': task,
        'model': model,
    });

    const pipelineClass = pipelineInfo.pipeline;
    return new pipelineClass(results);
}
```

**Key Workflow:**
1. Apply task aliases (e.g., "sentiment-analysis" → "text-classification")
2. Look up task in `SUPPORTED_TASKS` using first segment (before first underscore)
3. Use specified model or default from `SUPPORTED_TASKS[task].default.model`
4. Create a Map of tokenizer/model/processor classes from SUPPORTED_TASKS entry
5. Load items in parallel via `loadItems()` helper
6. Instantiate the pipeline class with loaded components

---

### 6. Pipeline Export Structure

**All exported pipeline classes (lines ~164-2890):**

Base class:
```javascript
export class Pipeline extends Callable { ... }
```

Text-based pipelines:
- `TextClassificationPipeline`
- `TokenClassificationPipeline`
- `QuestionAnsweringPipeline`
- `FillMaskPipeline`
- `Text2TextGenerationPipeline`
- `SummarizationPipeline`
- `TranslationPipeline`
- `TextGenerationPipeline`
- `ZeroShotClassificationPipeline`
- `FeatureExtractionPipeline`

Audio pipelines:
- `AudioClassificationPipeline`
- `ZeroShotAudioClassificationPipeline`
- `AutomaticSpeechRecognitionPipeline`

Multimodal pipelines:
- **`ImageToTextPipeline`** (uses TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs)
- `ImageClassificationPipeline`
- `ImageSegmentationPipeline`
- `BackgroundRemovalPipeline`
- `ZeroShotImageClassificationPipeline`
- `ObjectDetectionPipeline`
- `ZeroShotObjectDetectionPipeline`
- **`DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline`** (uses TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs)

Special pipelines:
- `ImageFeatureExtractionPipeline`
- `TextToAudioPipeline`
- `ImageToImagePipeline`
- `DepthEstimationPipeline`

Factory function:
```javascript
export async function pipeline(task, model = null, options = {}) { ... }
```

---

### 7. Constructor Args Type Hierarchy

**Text-only (line 193):**
```javascript
@typedef {ModelTokenizerConstructorArgs} TextPipelineConstructorArgs
```
Contains: `task`, `model`, `tokenizer`

**Image/Audio-only (lines 197-204):**
```javascript
@typedef {ModelProcessorConstructorArgs} ImagePipelineConstructorArgs
@typedef {ModelProcessorConstructorArgs} AudioPipelineConstructorArgs
```
Contains: `task`, `model`, `processor`

**Multimodal with Text+Audio (line 214):**
```javascript
@typedef {ModelTokenizerProcessorConstructorArgs} TextAudioPipelineConstructorArgs
```
Contains: `task`, `model`, `tokenizer`, `processor`

**Multimodal with Text+Image (line 215):**
```javascript
@typedef {ModelTokenizerProcessorConstructorArgs} TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs
```
Contains: `task`, `model`, `tokenizer`, `processor`

---

## Summary: Pattern for Adding a New Pipeline

To add a new pipeline following this architecture:

1. **Define Typedefs** (at pipeline location):
   - Output typedef (e.g., `MyPipelineSingle`, `MyPipelineOutput`)
   - Callback typedef with `@callback` and `@param` / `@returns`
   - Combined typedef using constructor args + callback + Disposable

2. **Create Class**:
   ```javascript
   export class MyPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: MyConstructorArgs) => MyPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {
       constructor(options) {
           super(options);
       }
       
       /** @type {MyPipelineCallback} */
       async _call(input, options = {}) {
           // Implementation
       }
   }
   ```

3. **Register in SUPPORTED_TASKS**:
   ```javascript
   "my-task": {
       "tokenizer": AutoTokenizer,  // if needed
       "pipeline": MyPipeline,
       "model": AutoModelForMyTask,
       "processor": AutoProcessor,  // if needed
       "default": {
           "model": "Xenova/my-model",
       },
       "type": "multimodal",  // or "text", "audio", "image"
   },
   ```

4. **Export the class** automatically via `export class MyPipeline...`

5. **JSDoc list** is maintained manually in the pipeline factory function JSDoc

 * @returns {Promise<DocumentQuestionAnsweringOutput|DocumentQuestionAnsweringOutput[]>} An object (or array of objects) containing the answer(s).
 *
 * @typedef {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs & DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineCallback & Disposable} DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineType
 */
```

**Class Declaration:**
```javascript
export class DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {

    /**
     * Create a new DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline.
     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
     */
    constructor(options) {
        super(options);
    }
```

**_call Method (lines 2742-2778):**
```javascript
    /** @type {DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineCallback} */
    async _call(image, question, generate_kwargs = {}) {

        // NOTE: For now, we only support a batch size of 1

        // Preprocess image
        const preparedImage = (await prepareImages(image))[0];
        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImage);

        // Run tokenization
        const task_prompt = `<s_docvqa><s_question>${question}</s_question><s_answer>`;
        const decoder_input_ids = this.tokenizer(task_prompt, {
            add_special_tokens: false,
            padding: true,
            truncation: true,
        }).input_ids;

        // Run model
        const output = await this.model.generate({
            inputs: pixel_values,
            // @ts-expect-error TS2339
            max_length: this.model.config.decoder.max_position_embeddings,
            decoder_input_ids,
            ...generate_kwargs,
        });

        // Decode output
        const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output))[0];

        // Parse answer
        const match = decoded.match(/<s_answer>(.*?)<\/s_answer>/);
        let answer = null;
        if (match && match.length >= 2) {
            answer = match[1].trim();
        }
        return [{ answer }];
    }
```

**Key Characteristics:**
- **Multimodal:** Takes both image and text (question) inputs
- **Constructor args type:** `TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs` (requires tokenizer AND processor)
- **Processing flow:** Image preprocessing → Processor for pixel values → Tokenizer for question prompt → Model generation → Regex parsing of answer
- **Single batch:** Only supports batch size of 1 (per comment)
- **Output:** Always returns array format `[{ answer: string }]`

### 3. IMAGETOTEXTPIPELINE - Complete Implementation

**Location:** Lines 1943-2005

 * @returns {Promise<ImageToTextOutput|ImageToTextOutput[]>} An object (or array of objects) containing the generated text(s).
 *
 * @typedef {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs & ImageToTextPipelineCallback & Disposable} ImageToTextPipelineType
 */
```

**Class Declaration:**

export class ImageToTextPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => ImageToTextPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {

    /**
     * Create a new ImageToTextPipeline.

```

**_call Method (lines 1985-2004):**
```javascript
    /** @type {ImageToTextPipelineCallback} */
    async _call(images, generate_kwargs = {}) {

        const isBatched = Array.isArray(images);
        const preparedImages = await prepareImages(images);

        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImages);

        const toReturn = [];
        for (const batch of pixel_values) {
            batch.dims = [1, ...batch.dims]
            const output = await this.model.generate({ inputs: batch, ...generate_kwargs });
            const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output), {
                skip_special_tokens: true,
            }).map(x => ({ generated_text: x.trim() }))
            toReturn.push(decoded);
        }

        return isBatched ? toReturn : toReturn[0];
    }
```

**Key Characteristics:**
- **Multimodal:** Takes images as input, generates text (captions/OCR)
- **Constructor args type:** `TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs` (requires tokenizer AND processor)
- **Batching:** Supports both single and batch inputs with `isBatched` flag
- **Processing flow:** Image preparation → Processor for pixel values → Model generation → Tokenizer batch decode
- **Output format:** Preserves batching (returns array for batched input, single object for single input)
- **Batch dimension:** Modifies `batch.dims` to add leading 1: `[1, ...batch.dims]`

### 4. SUPPORTED_TASKS Mapping Examples

**Location:** Lines 3096-3390

**Key Examples Showing Registration Pattern:**

**Text-Only Pipeline (text-classification):**
```javascript
"text-classification": {
    "tokenizer": AutoTokenizer,
    "pipeline": TextClassificationPipeline,
    "model": AutoModelForSequenceClassification,
    "default": {
        "model": "Xenova/distilbert-base-uncased-finetuned-sst-2-english",

    "type": "text",
},
```

**Audio-Only Pipeline (audio-classification):**

    "pipeline": AudioClassificationPipeline,
    "model": AutoModelForAudioClassification,
    "processor": AutoProcessor,
    "default": {
        "model": "Xenova/wav2vec2-base-superb-ks",
    },
    "type": "audio",
},
```

**Multimodal with Optional Components (text-to-audio):**
```javascript
"text-to-audio": {
    "tokenizer": AutoTokenizer,
    "pipeline": TextToAudioPipeline,
    "model": [AutoModelForTextToWaveform, AutoModelForTextToSpectrogram],
    "processor": [AutoProcessor, /* Some don't use a processor */ null],
    "default": {
        "model": "Xenova/speecht5_tts",
    },
    "type": "text",
},
```

**Reference: ImageToTextPipeline Registration:**
```javascript

    "tokenizer": AutoTokenizer,
    "pipeline": ImageToTextPipeline,

    },
    "type": "multimodal",
},
```

**Key Registration Patterns:**
- Entries are frozen with `Object.freeze()`
- Required properties: `pipeline` (class), `model` (class or array), `default` (object with model name), `type`
- Optional properties: `tokenizer`, `processor` (can be omitted, be an array, or include `null` for optionality)
- Type field: can be `"text"`, `"audio"`, `"image"`, or `"multimodal"`

### 5. Pipeline Factory Function

**Location:** Lines 3414-3513

**Complete JSDoc (from lines 3414-3444):**
```javascript
/**
 * Utility factory method to build a `Pipeline` object.
 *
 * @template {PipelineType} T The type of pipeline to return.
 * @param {T} task The task defining which pipeline will be returned. Currently accepted tasks are:
 *  - `"audio-classification"`: will return a `AudioClassificationPipeline`.
 *  - `"automatic-speech-recognition"`: will return a `AutomaticSpeechRecognitionPipeline`.
 *  - `"depth-estimation"`: will return a `DepthEstimationPipeline`.
 *  - `"document-question-answering"`: will return a `DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline`.
 *  - `"feature-extraction"`: will return a `FeatureExtractionPipeline`.
 *  - `"fill-mask"`: will return a `FillMaskPipeline`.
 *  - `"image-classification"`: will return a `ImageClassificationPipeline`.
 *  - `"image-segmentation"`: will return a `ImageSegmentationPipeline`.
 *  - `"image-to-text"`: will return a `ImageToTextPipeline`.
 *  - `"object-detection"`: will return a `ObjectDetectionPipeline`.
 *  - `"question-answering"`: will return a `QuestionAnsweringPipeline`.
 *  - `"summarization"`: will return a `SummarizationPipeline`.
 *  - `"text2text-generation"`: will return a `Text2TextGenerationPipeline`.
 *  - `"text-classification"` (alias "sentiment-analysis" available): will return a `TextClassificationPipeline`.
 *  - `"text-generation"`: will return a `TextGenerationPipeline`.
 *  - `"token-classification"` (alias "ner" available): will return a `TokenClassificationPipeline`.
 *  - `"translation"`: will return a `TranslationPipeline`.
 *  - `"translation_xx_to_yy"`: will return a `TranslationPipeline`.
 *  - `"zero-shot-classification"`: will return a `ZeroShotClassificationPipeline`.
 *  - `"zero-shot-audio-classification"`: will return a `ZeroShotAudioClassificationPipeline`.
 *  - `"zero-shot-image-classification"`: will return a `ZeroShotImageClassificationPipeline`.
 *  - `"zero-shot-object-detection"`: will return a `ZeroShotObjectDetectionPipeline`.
 * @param {string} [model=null] The name of the pre-trained model to use. If not specified, the default model for the task will be used.
 * @param {import('./utils/hub.js').PretrainedModelOptions} [options] Optional parameters for the pipeline.
 * @returns {Promise<AllTasks[T]>} A Pipeline object for the specified task.
 * @throws {Error} If an unsupported pipeline is requested.
 */
export async function pipeline(
    task,
    model = null,
    {
        progress_callback = null,
        config = null,
        cache_dir = null,
        local_files_only = false,
        revision = 'main',
        device = null,
        dtype = null,
        subfolder = 'onnx',
        use_external_data_format = null,
        model_file_name = null,
        session_options = {},
    } = {}
)
```

**Function Implementation (lines 3463-3513):**
```javascript
{
    // Helper method to construct pipeline

    // Apply aliases
    // @ts-ignore
    task = TASK_ALIASES[task] ?? task;

    // Get pipeline info
    const pipelineInfo = SUPPORTED_TASKS[task.split('_', 1)[0]];
    if (!pipelineInfo) {
        throw Error(`Unsupported pipeline: ${task}. Must be one of [${Object.keys(SUPPORTED_TASKS)}]`)
    }

    // Use model if specified, otherwise, use default
    if (!model) {
        model = pipelineInfo.default.model
        console.log(`No model specified. Using default model: "${model}".`);
    }

    const pretrainedOptions = {
        progress_callback,
        config,
        cache_dir,
        local_files_only,
        revision,
        device,
        dtype,
        subfolder,
        use_external_data_format,
        model_file_name,
        session_options,
    }

    const classes = new Map([
        ['tokenizer', pipelineInfo.tokenizer],
        ['model', pipelineInfo.model],
        ['processor', pipelineInfo.processor],
    ]);

    // Load model, tokenizer, and processor (if they exist)
    const results = await loadItems(classes, model, pretrainedOptions);
    results.task = task;

    dispatchCallback(progress_callback, {
        'status': 'ready',
        'task': task,
        'model': model,
    });

    const pipelineClass = pipelineInfo.pipeline;
    return new pipelineClass(results);
}
```

**Key Workflow:**
1. Apply task aliases (e.g., "sentiment-analysis" → "text-classification")
2. Look up task in `SUPPORTED_TASKS` using first segment (before first underscore)
3. Use specified model or default from `SUPPORTED_TASKS[task].default.model`
4. Create a Map of tokenizer/model/processor classes from SUPPORTED_TASKS entry
5. Load items in parallel via `loadItems()` helper
6. Instantiate the pipeline class with loaded components

```

```

```
Contains: `task`, `model`, `tokenizer`, `processor`

## Summary: Pattern for Adding a New Pipeline

To add a new pipeline following this architecture:

1. **Define Typedefs** (at pipeline location):
   - Output typedef (e.g., `MyPipelineSingle`, `MyPipelineOutput`)
   - Callback typedef with `@callback` and `@param` / `@returns`
   - Combined typedef using constructor args + callback + Disposable

2. **Create Class**:
   ```javascript
   export class MyPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: MyConstructorArgs) => MyPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {
       constructor(options) {
           super(options);
       }
       
       /** @type {MyPipelineCallback} */
       async _call(input, options = {}) {
           // Implementation
       }
   }
   ```

3. **Register in SUPPORTED_TASKS**:
   ```javascript
   "my-task": {
       "tokenizer": AutoTokenizer,  // if needed
       "pipeline": MyPipeline,
       "model": AutoModelForMyTask,
       "processor": AutoProcessor,  // if needed
       "default": {
           "model": "Xenova/my-model",
       },
       "type": "multimodal",  // or "text", "audio", "image"
   },
   ```

4. **Export the class** automatically via `export class MyPipeline...`

5. **JSDoc list** is maintained manually in the pipeline factory function JSDoc

## Summary

I've thoroughly explored the Transformers.js codebase and found comprehensive information about `AutoModelForImageTextToText` and related auto model classes. Here are the key findings:

### 1. **AutoModelForImageTextToText Definition**

Location: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775400268200-c01bcp/src/models.js` (lines 8736-8738)

```javascript
export class AutoModelForImageTextToText extends PretrainedMixin {
    static MODEL_CLASS_MAPPINGS = [MODEL_FOR_IMAGE_TEXT_TO_TEXT_MAPPING_NAMES];
}
```

**What it does:** `AutoModelForImageTextToText` is a helper class that instantiates pretrained image-text-to-text models. The actual model class is determined by examining the `model_type` in the model configuration. This is a factory/auto-loading pattern that automatically selects the appropriate model class based on the config.

### 2. **Supported Models in MODEL_FOR_IMAGE_TEXT_TO_TEXT_MAPPING_NAMES**

Lines 8240-8252:
- `llava` - LlavaForConditionalGeneration
- `llava_onevision` - LlavaOnevisionForConditionalGeneration
- `moondream1` - Moondream1ForConditionalGeneration
- `florence2` - Florence2ForConditionalGeneration
- `qwen2-vl` - Qwen2VLForConditionalGeneration
- `idefics3` - Idefics3ForConditionalGeneration
- `smolvlm` - SmolVLMForConditionalGeneration
- `paligemma` - PaliGemmaForConditionalGeneration
- `llava_qwen2` - LlavaQwen2ForCausalLM
- `gemma3n` - Gemma3nForConditionalGeneration
- `mistral3` - Mistral3ForConditionalGeneration

### 3. **Available Methods (generate())**

Models loaded via `AutoModelForImageTextToText.from_pretrained()` inherit all methods from their parent class hierarchy and can call the **`generate()`** method. This is enabled because:

- `ImageTextToText` models are registered with `MODEL_TYPES.ImageTextToText = 6` (line 133)
- In the PreTrainedModel constructor (lines 1044-1048), when model type is `ImageTextToText`, the model is configured with:
  - `this.can_generate = true`
  - `this._forward = imageTextToTextForward` - the forward function
  - `this._prepare_inputs_for_generation = multimodal_text_to_text_prepare_inputs_for_generation` - handles input preparation

**Key methods on models:**
- **`generate(inputs, generation_config, ...)`** - Primary generation method (line 1735+)
  - Takes `inputs` (typically from processor output)
  - Accepts `max_new_tokens`, `do_sample`, `return_dict_in_generate`, and other generation parameters
  - Returns tensor(s) of generated token IDs
  
- **`from_pretrained(model_name, options)`** - Loads the model (inherited from PretrainedMixin, lines 7858+)

- **`encode_image(pixel_values)`** - Encodes image inputs (line 2029)

- **`_merge_input_ids_with_image_features(kwargs)`** - Merges image features with text embeddings (implemented in subclasses like LlavaForConditionalGeneration, line 3705)

### 4. **Comparison with AutoModelForVision2Seq**

Lines 8617-8619:
```javascript
export class AutoModelForVision2Seq extends PretrainedMixin {
    static MODEL_CLASS_MAPPINGS = [MODEL_FOR_VISION_2_SEQ_MAPPING_NAMES];
}
```

**Similarities:**
- Both extend `PretrainedMixin` for the same `from_pretrained()` interface
- Both support `generate()` method
- Both handle multimodal (vision + text) inputs

**Key Differences:**
- **Vision2Seq** (lines 8234-8238) supports: vision-encoder-decoder, idefics3, smolvlm
- **Vision2Seq** uses `MODEL_TYPES.Vision2Seq = 3` (line 130)
- **ImageTextToText** uses `MODEL_TYPES.ImageTextToText = 6` (line 133)
- **Vision2Seq** typically represents encoder-decoder architectures (image encoder + text decoder)
- **ImageTextToText** represents models that can work as either encoder-decoder or causal LM (line 943-948)
- **ImageTextToText** forward uses `imageTextToTextForward` function with the generic `genericTextToTextForward` helper (lines 819-827)
- **Vision2Seq** forward uses `seq2seqForward`

### 5. **Forward Pass Details for ImageTextToText**

The forward process for image-text-to-text models (lines 819-827 and 718-793):

```javascript
async function imageTextToTextForward(self, params) {
    return await genericTextToTextForward(self, {
        ...params,
        modality_input_name: 'pixel_values',
        modality_output_name: 'image_features',
        encode_function: self.encode_image.bind(self),
        merge_function: self._merge_input_ids_with_image_features.bind(self),
    });
}
```

**Steps in genericTextToTextForward:**
1. Encodes text embeddings via `self.encode_text()`
2. Encodes image features via `self.encode_image()`
3. Merges image features with text embeddings via `self._merge_input_ids_with_image_features()`
4. Calls decoder forward pass on merged embeddings

### 6. **Exports**

The `AutoModelForImageTextToText` class is exported via:
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775400268200-c01bcp/src/models.js` (direct export)
- Re-exported through `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775400268200-c01bcp/src/transformers.js` via `export * from './models.js'`

All AutoModel classes follow the same export pattern.

### 7. **Existing References to Image-Text-to-Text**

Found in three main files:
- **`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775400268200-c01bcp/src/models.js`** - Core implementation (multiple locations as documented above)
- **`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775400268200-c01bcp/tests/models/llava/test_modeling_llava.js`** - Test file showing usage examples
- **`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775400268200-c01bcp/tests/utils/generation.test.js`** - Generation tests using LlavaForConditionalGeneration

The feature is **fully implemented**, not partial. The auto class, model type, forward functions, and model implementations all exist and are production-ready.

- **ImageTextToText** uses `MODEL_TYPES.ImageTextToText = 6` (line 133)
- **Vision2Seq** typically represents encoder-decoder architectures (image encoder + text decoder)
- **ImageTextToText** represents models that can work as either encoder-decoder or causal LM (line 943-948)
- **ImageTextToText** forward uses `imageTextToTextForward` function with the generic `genericTextToTextForward` helper (lines 819-827)
- **Vision2Seq** forward uses `seq2seqForward`

### 5. **Forward Pass Details for ImageTextToText**

The forward process for image-text-to-text models (lines 819-827 and 718-793):

```javascript
async function imageTextToTextForward(self, params) {
    return await genericTextToTextForward(self, {
        ...params,
        modality_input_name: 'pixel_values',
        modality_output_name: 'image_features',
        encode_function: self.encode_image.bind(self),
        merge_function: self._merge_input_ids_with_image_features.bind(self),
    });
}
```

**Steps in genericTextToTextForward:**
1. Encodes text embeddings via `self.encode_text()`
2. Encodes image features via `self.encode_image()`
3. Merges image features with text embeddings via `self._merge_input_ids_with_image_features()`
4. Calls decoder forward pass on merged embeddings

## File: src/pipelines.js

### Imports (lines 15-76)

- `AutoModelForImageTextToText` exists in `src/models.js` (lines 8736-8738) and supports models like LLaVA, Florence-2, Qwen2-VL, etc.

- `ImageInput` = `string | RawImage | URL | Blob | HTMLCanvasElement | OffscreenCanvas` (line 81)
- `ImagePipelineInputs` = `ImageInput | ImageInput[]` (line 82)
- `TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs` = `ModelTokenizerProcessorConstructorArgs` (line 215) — includes task, model, tokenizer, processor

- `_call(images, generate_kwargs = {})`: processes images via processor, generates, decodes with tokenizer
- Returns `[{ generated_text: string }]`

- JSDoc typedefs at lines 2704-2716
- Class at line 2732, extends Pipeline with TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs  
- `_call(image, question, generate_kwargs = {})`: takes image + text, processes both, generates, decodes
- Returns `[{ answer: string }]`

### SUPPORTED_TASKS (lines 3096-3390)
- `"image-to-text"` entry at lines 3244-3255: tokenizer, pipeline, model (AutoModelForVision2Seq), processor, default model, type "multimodal"
- `"document-question-answering"` entry at lines 3315-3326

### Pipeline factory JSDoc (lines 3414-3440)
- Lists all supported tasks in alphabetical order. `"image-text-to-text"` would go after `"image-to-text"` (line 3427)

### Pipeline class ends at line 2779 (DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline), then TextToAudioPipeline starts at line 2782

## What AutoModelForImageTextToText does
- Defined in src/models.js, supports models: llava, florence2, qwen2-vl, idefics3, smolvlm, paligemma, etc.
- Has `can_generate = true`, uses `imageTextToTextForward` which processes pixel_values + input_ids
- The `generate()` method accepts an object with `inputs` (pixel_values), input_ids, attention_mask, etc.

## The new pipeline needs to:
1. Accept an image + text prompt (e.g., "Describe this image")
2. Process the image via processor to get pixel_values (and possibly other image features)
3. Tokenize the text prompt to get input_ids
4. Call `this.model.generate()` with both pixel_values and input_ids
5. Decode generated tokens back to text
6. Return `[{ generated_text: string }]`

## Key difference from ImageToTextPipeline:
- ImageToTextPipeline only takes images as input, no text prompt
- ImageTextToTextPipeline takes BOTH image AND text as input (like DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline but for general text generation)

## Key difference from DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline:
- DocQA uses a very specific prompt format (`<s_docvqa>...`) and model-specific generation config
- ImageTextToText should be more generic — pass the text prompt directly via tokenizer, let the processor handle chat template application

## For ImageTextToText models, the processor often handles both image and text together
Looking at how these models work in the Python Transformers library, the processor is typically called with BOTH images and text, producing pixel_values AND input_ids together. So for this pipeline, the processor should handle both.

However, looking at the JS codebase patterns more carefully, the existing pipelines call processor for images and tokenizer separately. For ImageTextToText models specifically, we should check what the processor's `_call` method handles.

Given the JS codebase pattern and that AutoProcessor for vision-language models can handle images, while the tokenizer handles text, I should design the `_call` method to:
1. Process image with processor → pixel_values
2. Tokenize text with tokenizer → input_ids  
3. Generate with model passing both
4. Decode output

Design a precise implementation plan including exact code for the new ImageTextToTextPipeline class, its JSDoc, SUPPORTED_TASKS entry, import addition, and factory function JSDoc update. Use `"onnx-community/Florence-2-base-ft"` as the default model.

1	/**
2	 * @file Pipelines provide a high-level, easy to use, API for running machine learning models.
3	 *
4	 * **Example:** Instantiate pipeline using the `pipeline` function.
5	 * ```javascript
6	 * import { pipeline } from '@huggingface/transformers';
7	 *
8	 * const classifier = await pipeline('sentiment-analysis');
9	 * const output = await classifier('I love transformers!');
10	 * // [{'label': 'POSITIVE', 'score': 0.999817686}]
11	 * ```

112	async function prepareAudios(audios, sampling_rate) {
113	    if (!Array.isArray(audios)) {
114	        audios = [audios];
115	    }
116

118	        if (typeof x === 'string' || x instanceof URL) {

744	        inputs_embeds = await self.encode_text({ input_ids, ...kwargs });

- `"document-question-answering"` entry at lines 3315-3326

### Pipeline class ends at line 2779 (DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline), then TextToAudioPipeline starts at line 2782

## What AutoModelForImageTextToText does
- Defined in src/models.js, supports models: llava, florence2, qwen2-vl, idefics3, smolvlm, paligemma, etc.
- Has `can_generate = true`, uses `imageTextToTextForward` which processes pixel_values + input_ids
- The `generate()` method accepts an object with `inputs` (pixel_values), input_ids, attention_mask, etc.

## The new pipeline needs to:
1. Accept an image + text prompt (e.g., "Describe this image")
2. Process the image via processor to get pixel_values (and possibly other image features)
3. Tokenize the text prompt to get input_ids
4. Call `this.model.generate()` with both pixel_values and input_ids
5. Decode generated tokens back to text
6. Return `[{ generated_text: string }]`

## Key difference from ImageToTextPipeline:
- ImageToTextPipeline only takes images as input, no text prompt
- ImageTextToTextPipeline takes BOTH image AND text as input (like DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline but for general text generation)

## Key difference from DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline:
- DocQA uses a very specific prompt format (`<s_docvqa>...`) and model-specific generation config
- ImageTextToText should be more generic — pass the text prompt directly via tokenizer, let the processor handle chat template application

## For ImageTextToText models, the processor often handles both image and text together
Looking at how these models work in the Python Transformers library, the processor is typically called with BOTH images and text, producing pixel_values AND input_ids together. So for this pipeline, the processor should handle both.

However, looking at the JS codebase patterns more carefully, the existing pipelines call processor for images and tokenizer separately. For ImageTextToText models specifically, we should check what the processor's `_call` method handles.

Given the JS codebase pattern and that AutoProcessor for vision-language models can handle images, while the tokenizer handles text, I should design the `_call` method to:
1. Process image with processor → pixel_values
2. Tokenize text with tokenizer → input_ids  
3. Generate with model passing both
4. Decode output

input.content:
# Plan: Add `image-text-to-text` Pipeline to Transformers.js

## Context

The Transformers.js library already has `AutoModelForImageTextToText` in `src/models.js` (supporting LLaVA, Florence-2, Qwen2-VL, Idefics3, SmolVLM, PaliGemma, etc.), but no corresponding pipeline exists. This change adds an `ImageTextToTextPipeline` class so users can run image+text-to-text generation via the high-level `pipeline()` API. All changes are in `src/pipelines.js`.

---

## Changes (all in `src/pipelines.js`)

### 1. Add import for `AutoModelForImageTextToText` (~line 43)

Insert `AutoModelForImageTextToText` into the model import block from `'./models.js'`, between `AutoModelForImageFeatureExtraction` and `PreTrainedModel`:

```js
    AutoModelForImageFeatureExtraction,
    AutoModelForImageTextToText,
    PreTrainedModel,
```

### 2. Add `ImageTextToTextPipeline` class with JSDoc (after line 2005, between `ImageToTextPipeline` and `ImageClassificationPipeline`)

**JSDoc typedefs** (following the exact pattern of `ImageToTextPipeline` at lines 1943-1954 and `DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline` at lines 2704-2716):

```js
/**
 * @typedef {Object} ImageTextToTextSingle
 * @property {string} generated_text The generated text.
 * @typedef {ImageTextToTextSingle[]} ImageTextToTextOutput
 *
 * @callback ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback Generate text based on an image and a text prompt.
 * @param {ImagePipelineInputs} images The image(s) to use as context.
 * @param {string|string[]} [text] The text prompt(s) describing what to do with the image.
 * @param {Partial<import('./generation/configuration_utils.js').GenerationConfig>} [options] Additional keyword arguments to pass along to the generate method of the model.
 * @returns {Promise<ImageTextToTextOutput|ImageTextToTextOutput[]>} An object (or array of objects) containing the generated text(s).
 *
 * @typedef {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs & ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback & Disposable} ImageTextToTextPipelineType
 */
```

**Class-level JSDoc** with example usage:

```js
/**
 * Image-Text-to-Text pipeline using a `AutoModelForImageTextToText`. This pipeline generates
 * text given an image and an optional text prompt.
 *
 * **Example:** Generate text from an image and a prompt with `onnx-community/Florence-2-base-ft`.
 * ```javascript
 * const generator = await pipeline('image-text-to-text', 'onnx-community/Florence-2-base-ft');
 * const url = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/cats.jpg';
 * const output = await generator(url, 'Describe this image in detail');
 * // [{ generated_text: '...' }]
 * ```
 */
```

**Class implementation** — uses `TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs` (tokenizer + processor + model), same pattern as `ImageToTextPipeline`:

```js
export class ImageTextToTextPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => ImageTextToTextPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {

        const isBatched = Array.isArray(images);

        // Tokenize text prompt if provided
        const text_inputs = text
            ? this.tokenizer(text, { padding: true, truncation: true })
            : {};

        const toReturn = [];

/**
 * @file Pipelines provide a high-level, easy to use, API for running machine learning models.
 *
 * **Example:** Instantiate pipeline using the `pipeline` function.
 * ```javascript
 * import { pipeline } from '@huggingface/transformers';
 *
 * const classifier = await pipeline('sentiment-analysis');
 * const output = await classifier('I love transformers!');
 * // [{'label': 'POSITIVE', 'score': 0.999817686}]
 * ```
 *
 * @module pipelines
 */

import {
    AutoTokenizer,
    PreTrainedTokenizer,
} from './tokenizers.js';

    AutoModelForZeroShotObjectDetection,
    AutoModelForDocumentQuestionAnswering,
    AutoModelForImageToImage,
    AutoModelForDepthEstimation,
    AutoModelForImageFeatureExtraction,
    PreTrainedModel,
} from './models.js';
import {
    AutoProcessor,
} from './models/auto/processing_auto.js';

    Processor,

3414	/**
3415	 * Utility factory method to build a `Pipeline` object.
3416	 *
3417	 * @template {PipelineType} T The type of pipeline to return.
3418	 * @param {T} task The task defining which pipeline will be returned. Currently accepted tasks are:
3419	 *  - `"audio-classification"`: will return a `AudioClassificationPipeline`.
3420	 *  - `"automatic-speech-recognition"`: will return a `AutomaticSpeechRecognitionPipeline`.
3421	 *  - `"depth-estimation"`: will return a `DepthEstimationPipeline`.
3422	 *  - `"document-question-answering"`: will return a `DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline`.
3423	 *  - `"feature-extraction"`: will return a `FeatureExtractionPipeline`.
3424	 *  - `"fill-mask"`: will return a `FillMaskPipeline`.
3425	 *  - `"image-classification"`: will return a `ImageClassificationPipeline`.
3426	 *  - `"image-segmentation"`: will return a `ImageSegmentationPipeline`.
3427	 *  - `"image-to-text"`: will return a `ImageToTextPipeline`.
3428	 *  - `"object-detection"`: will return a `ObjectDetectionPipeline`.
3429	 *  - `"question-answering"`: will return a `QuestionAnsweringPipeline`.
3430	 *  - `"summarization"`: will return a `SummarizationPipeline`.
3431	 *  - `"text2text-generation"`: will return a `Text2TextGenerationPipeline`.
3432	 *  - `"text-classification"` (alias "sentiment-analysis" available): will return a `TextClassificationPipeline`.
3433	 *  - `"text-generation"`: will return a `TextGenerationPipeline`.
3434	 *  - `"token-classification"` (alias "ner" available): will return a `TokenClassificationPipeline`.
3435	 *  - `"translation"`: will return a `TranslationPipeline`.
3436	 *  - `"translation_xx_to_yy"`: will return a `TranslationPipeline`.
3437	 *  - `"zero-shot-classification"`: will return a `ZeroShotClassificationPipeline`.
3438	 *  - `"zero-shot-audio-classification"`: will return a `ZeroShotAudioClassificationPipeline`.
3439	 *  - `"zero-shot-image-classification"`: will return a `ZeroShotImageClassificationPipeline`.
3440	 *  - `"zero-shot-object-detection"`: will return a `ZeroShotObjectDetectionPipeline`.
3441	 * @param {string} [model=null] The name of the pre-trained model to use. If not specified, the default model for the task will be used.
3442	 * @param {import('./utils/hub.js').PretrainedModelOptions} [options] Optional parameters for the pipeline.
3443	 * @returns {Promise<AllTasks[T]>} A Pipeline object for the specified task.
3444	 * @throws {Error} If an unsupported pipeline is requested.
3445	 */

3470	    const pipelineInfo = SUPPORTED_TASKS[task.split('_', 1)[0]];
